sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value FLOAT, timestamp_value TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, timestamp_value) VALUES (1, 5.0, timestamp '2021-01-01 10:00:00'), (2, 3.5, timestamp '2021-02-01 11:00:00'), (3, NULL, timestamp '2021-03-01 12:00:00');
SELECT id, mod(value, 2) AS mod_value, timestamp_value FROM test_table WHERE mod(value, NULL) IS NULL;
SELECT id, value, mod(value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table)) AS mod_count FROM test_table;
SELECT id, mod(value, mod(value, 2)) AS double_mod_value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, mod(value, 2.0) AS mod_value FROM test_table WHERE timestamp_value > timestamp '2021-01-01 00:00:00';
CREATE TABLE other_table (id INT, related_id INT, related_timestamp TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO other_table (id, related_id, related_timestamp) VALUES (1, 1, timestamp '2022-01-01 00:00:00'), (2, 2, timestamp '2022-02-01 00:00:00');
SELECT t1.id, mod(t1.value, 2) AS mod_value FROM test_table t1 JOIN other_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.related_id WHERE mod(t1.value, 2.5) < 1;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING mod(COUNT(*), 2) = 0;
SELECT id, mod(value, (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL)) AS mod_max_value FROM test_table WHERE mod(value, 1) > 0;
